% GENERATED FROM reports/manuscript/methods_and_results.qmd — DO NOT EDIT BY HAND.
% Edit the prose in the .qmd, then: quarto render reports/manuscript

\section{Methods}\label{methods}

We analysed a simulated consumer decision-making dataset of 300
participants, each randomly assigned to one of 5 message-framing
conditions (a plain control plus scarcity, sustainability, social-proof,
and combined frames). After viewing the framed product, participants
reported multi-item measures of price sensitivity, sustainability
concern, brand trust, and perceived value, followed by their purchase
intention and a hypothetical buy decision.

Multi-item scales were averaged into composite scores after
reverse-scoring the relevant brand-trust item, and a value of \(-99\)
was treated as missing. Data were cleaned with scripted,
version-controlled steps; the full codebook and cleaning code live in
the project repository. All models were estimated in R with ordinary
least squares (\texttt{lm}). Every figure and table in this paper is
generated directly from the analysis code, so the reported numbers
cannot drift from the analysis.

\section{Results}\label{results}

\subsection{Main effect}\label{main-effect}

Relative to the control frame, the combined frame raised purchase
intention by 0.58 points on the seven-point scale (\(p = 0.008\)).
Figure~\ref{fig-pi-by-condition} shows the full pattern across
conditions.

\begin{figure}

\centering{

\includegraphics{methods_and_results_files/figure-latex/fig-pi-by-condition-1.pdf}

}

\caption{\label{fig-pi-by-condition}Purchase intention by condition. Red
points are condition means with 95\% confidence intervals.}

\end{figure}%

\subsection{Mediation through perceived
value}\label{mediation-through-perceived-value}

The frames also shifted perceived value upward, and perceived value
strongly predicted purchase intention
(Figure~\ref{fig-mediation-scatter}); adding it to the outcome model
attenuated the condition coefficients, the signature of an indirect
path.

\begin{figure}

\centering{

\includegraphics{methods_and_results_files/figure-latex/fig-mediation-scatter-1.pdf}

}

\caption{\label{fig-mediation-scatter}Perceived value predicts purchase
intention; the frames shift perceived value upward, producing an
indirect path.}

\end{figure}%

\subsection{Moderation by price
sensitivity}\label{moderation-by-price-sensitivity}

The combined-frame boost was attenuated among the most price-sensitive
respondents (Figure~\ref{fig-moderation}).

\begin{figure}

\centering{

\includegraphics{methods_and_results_files/figure-latex/fig-moderation-1.pdf}

}

\caption{\label{fig-moderation}Condition \(\times\) price sensitivity:
the combined-frame boost shrinks for high-price-sensitive respondents.}

\end{figure}%

\subsection{Model estimates}\label{model-estimates}

Table~\ref{tbl-models} collects the coefficients behind the figures
above. It is generated by
\texttt{reports/manuscript/methods\_and\_results.qmd} straight from the
fitted models, so the paper's numbers track the analysis exactly.
